provenance_note: Same compilation rules as Release B (Addendum A.1). New tiers per Addendum B: doctrines (theory-first corpora) and narratives (lore/conspiracy complexes with factual_kernel vs lore_layer split). Hoaxes are ordinary observation records with M-ART-FAB/M-ART-AUF resolution edges. record_kind distinguishes descriptions, demonstrations, observations, analyses.

instances.label: (see base corpus ex:bhaskara-wheel — root of the medieval lineage below)
instances.note: Pointer record only; kept for lineage readability in this file.

instances.label: Peregrinus perpetual magnetic wheel design
instances.note: Epistola de magnete closes with a lodestone-driven wheel intended to turn perpetually — the root document of the magnet-motor lineage, seven centuries before Perendev.

instances.label: Villard de Honnecourt overbalanced wheel
instances.note: Sketchbook wheel with hinged hammers, asserted to run of itself — the European entry of the overbalanced-wheel idea.

instances.label: Zimara self-blowing windmill proposal
instances.note: Windmill driving bellows that blow the windmill — the closed-loop archetype in its purest form.

instances.label: Fludd closed-cycle water mill
instances.note: Recirculating water-screw mill grinding grain from its own outflow; endlessly reprinted ancestor of hydraulic self-runner claims.

instances.label: Leonardo's refutation of overbalanced wheels
instances.note: Notebook analyses working the torque balance and dismissing the seekers of perpetual motion — the earliest analysis-type resolution record in the corpus.

instances.label: Drebbel perpetuum mobile at the Stuart court
instances.note: Celebrated self-moving globe presented to James I; modern reading: barometric/thermal drive — Cox's clock avant la lettre, an apparent self-runner with a real energy source.

instances.label: Marquess of Worcester wheel demonstration
instances.note: Large weighted wheel reportedly shown before the king at the Tower of London; known through the inventor's Century of Inventions and court lore.

instances.label: Sepulchral ever-burning lamp reports
instances.principals: Renaissance antiquarian tradition (compiled by Fortunio Liceti)
instances.note: Recurring reports of lamps found alight in opened ancient tombs (the Tulliola account among them); the medieval-to-early-modern free-energy claim par excellence, surviving only as secondary reports.

instances.label: Redheffer perpetual motion machine
instances.note: Paid-admission perpetual wheel; a Philadelphia inspection already noticed gear wear running the wrong way.

instances.label: Fulton's exposure of the Redheffer machine
instances.note: Fulton traced an uneven running rhythm to a concealed catgut belt and an elderly man cranking in an upper room — the template hoax exposure, ninety years before Wood's prism.

instances.label: Garabed free-energy claim before the US Congress
instances.note: Congress passed a joint resolution to examine the 'Garabed'; the committee identified it as a flywheel storing, not generating, energy — a legislative-branch P4 record.

instances.label: Cook electro-magnetic self-acting battery
instances.note: Community-flagged instance. Patented coil pair claimed to sustain its own current — the earliest US patent document in the electromagnetic self-runner lineage; compiler's recall dates it 1871, not 1895.

instances.label: Gary neutral-line magnetic motor
instances.note: 'Neutral line' magnet motor with sympathetic national-press coverage; the 19th-century prototype of the magnet-motor media wave.

instances.label: Pogue high-mileage vapor carburetor
instances.note: Patented vapor carburetors with ~200-mpg claims; never validated under controls; seed of the suppressed-carburetor narrative (nar:pogue-200mpg).

instances.label: Tewari reactionless generator claims
instances.note: Ex-nuclear-engineering executive; over-unity homopolar-style generator claims framed by his space-vortex theory (doc:tewari-space-vortex).

doctrines.label: Haramein holofractographic unified theory
doctrines.summary: Schwarzschild-proton and vacuum-geometry papers proposing a unified field; presented largely outside peer-reviewed venues.
doctrines.note: Theory-first corpus; no bench-observation records to link.

doctrines.label: Heim multidimensional field theory
doctrines.summary: Six/eight-dimensional structured field theory with a claimed particle-mass formula; propulsion implications discussed posthumously.
doctrines.note: Pure theory after Heim's 1944 laboratory accident; no observation corpus of his own.

doctrines.label: Shipov physical-vacuum / torsion theory
doctrines.summary: Theory of the physical vacuum underpinning the torsion-field program.
doctrines.note: The experimental wing is recorded separately; this entry is the doctrine.

doctrines.label: Bearden scalar electromagnetics
doctrines.summary: Scalar-wave and vacuum-engineering corpus (Energy from the Vacuum) framing MEG-type devices.
doctrines.note: Doctrine layer of the M-ZPE/M-SCW device cluster.

doctrines.label: Mills Grand Unified Theory of Classical Physics
doctrines.summary: Classical-physics reformulation predicting hydrino states below the hydrogen ground state.
doctrines.note: The theory behind the X-HYD private-extension mechanism.

doctrines.label: Larson Reciprocal System
doctrines.summary: Motion-as-fundamental cosmology with a persistent study community.

doctrines.label: LaViolette subquantum kinetics
doctrines.summary: Reaction-diffusion ether model; author also popularized electrogravitics-in-aerospace claims.
doctrines.note: Crosslinks narratively to the electrogravitics lore around ex:brown-gravitator-1929.

doctrines.label: Correa aetherometry
doctrines.summary: Massfree-energy framework built atop the PAGD experimental line.
doctrines.note: Doctrine layer of an experiment-bearing program.

doctrines.label: Hoerbiger World Ice Theory
doctrines.summary: Cosmic-ice cosmology with mass following in interwar Central Europe.
doctrines.note: Previously listed under omissions; now referenceable as doctrine — no discrete observations, by design of this tier.

doctrines.label: Tewari space vortex theory
doctrines.summary: Vacuum-vortex model of the electron motivating reactionless-generator work.
doctrines.note: Doctrine-instance pair with the RLG record.

narratives.label: Philadelphia Experiment
narratives.emergence: 1955 (Allende letters to M. K. Jessup); book wave 1979
narratives.narrative_provenance: Carl Allen/'Carlos Allende' correspondence and annotated Jessup volume; Berlitz & Moore popularization; ONR public denials.
narratives.factual_kernel: USS Eldridge existed; wartime degaussing research against magnetic mines was real.
narratives.lore_layer: Optical invisibility, teleportation to Norfolk, fused crewmen.

narratives.label: Die Glocke (Nazi bell device)
narratives.narrative_provenance: Claimed unpublished interrogation sources cited by Witkowski; no primary-period documents produced.
narratives.factual_kernel: SS technical programs and the Wenceslas-mine area exist.
narratives.lore_layer: Bell-shaped antigravity/time device, Xerum-525, Kecksburg tie-in.

narratives.label: Nazi-UFO / Vril complex
narratives.narrative_provenance: Postwar individual claims of saucer projects; Pauwels/Bergier and later channelled 'Vril Society' literature.
narratives.factual_kernel: Advanced conventional German aeronautics existed; the 'Vril Society' itself lacks primary-period evidence.
narratives.lore_layer: Flight discs, Thule/Vril occult engineering, Antarctic bases.

narratives.label: Tesla suppressed-papers narrative
narratives.emergence: from 1943; recurrent waves with FOIA releases
narratives.narrative_provenance: Office of Alien Property custody of Tesla's effects; public FBI file; John G. Trump's review memorandum.
narratives.factual_kernel: Custody of the estate and a government review are documented; the review found nothing operational.
narratives.lore_layer: Hidden death-ray and free-energy technology withheld from humanity.

narratives.label: Suppressed-inventor master narrative
narratives.narrative_provenance: Retold across the free-energy literature; each generation adds its martyr.
narratives.factual_kernel: Moray reported attacks on his lab; Meyer died suddenly in 1998 with death officially attributed to natural causes; several inventors faced real legal action (documented in the respective records).
narratives.lore_layer: A coordinated cartel systematically silencing working devices.

narratives.label: Suppressed 200-mpg carburetor
narratives.narrative_provenance: Newspaper claims around Pogue's devices; oral automotive folklore.
narratives.factual_kernel: Pogue's patents and mileage claims are documented.
narratives.lore_layer: Oil interests bought and buried the device.

narratives.label: HAARP weather/mind-control claims
narratives.narrative_provenance: Activist books and broadcast culture around the Gakona facility.
narratives.factual_kernel: HAARP is a real ionospheric research heater, now university-operated, with published science.
narratives.lore_layer: Weather weaponization, earthquake induction, mass mind control.

narratives.label: Hutchison equipment-seizure lore
narratives.factual_kernel: Hutchison reports confiscations of his apparatus; delegation interest in the 1980s is documented.
narratives.lore_layer: State suppression of a working effect.

narratives.label: Montauk Project
narratives.narrative_provenance: Book-driven; derivative continuation of the Philadelphia narrative.
narratives.lore_layer: Time tunnels, psychic chair, child experiments.

narratives.label: Free-energy patent-suppression narrative
narratives.narrative_provenance: Community readings of the US Invention Secrecy Act and annual secrecy-order counts.
narratives.factual_kernel: The Invention Secrecy Act (1951) and standing secrecy orders are real and documented via FOIA; the USPTO's historical working-model demand for perpetual-motion filings is real.
narratives.lore_layer: Systematic burial of functioning energy patents.
